evals.prompt: Create a workflow for onboarding a new vendor in our procurement system
evals.expected_output: Mode A: Runs init_workflow.py to scaffold the wrapper + content template, fills in frontmatter (workflow, slug, description, use, date), and writes the 5-phase Operation section (Initialize, Plan, Apply, Verify, Deliver) with vendor-onboarding-specific steps.

evals.prompt: I need a step-by-step workflow for rotating PDF files
evals.expected_output: Mode A: Triggers the skill, scaffolds a workflow (not a skill), uses the Template/Wrapper pattern, and produces a wrapper .md.tmpl with frontmatter + includeTemplate and a content template with rotation steps.

evals.prompt: Fix the typo in line 42 of src/index.ts
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger the skill — this is a one-off bug fix, not a reusable multi-step workflow. If triggered, the trigger-guard should answer directly and offer a rerun on 'go'.

evals.prompt: Convert my existing vendor-onboarding skill back into a workflow since it doesn't need scripts anymore
evals.expected_output: Mode B: Verifies the skill doesn't rely on scripts/references/evals/assets, git mv's SKILL.md to the wrapper path, commits the pure rename, then applies frontmatter conversion (skill→workflow schema) and structure changes (move body to content template) as separate commits.

evals.prompt: Audit and improve my existing ai-agent-create workflow
evals.expected_output: Mode C: Reads the existing wrapper + content template fully, audits frontmatter/description/step-structure/context-declaration/includes/stale-text, proposes prioritized changes (Critical/Important/Nice-to-have) with before/after, asks for confirmation before applying, applies approved changes as separate commits, updates date.updated and date.last-used.

evals.prompt: Convert my existing vendor-onboarding workflow into a skill so I can add evals and scripts
evals.expected_output: Does NOT trigger this skill — redirects to ai-skill-upsert (Mode B: Convert Workflow to a Skill) which handles the git-mv history preservation and skill-format transformation. This is the reverse of this skill's Mode B.
